content:
Hello!
Do we have procfs, abstractions in Rust? I may be blind, but it seems to me, that there is none.

From the RFL GitHub PRs ([1], [2]), it said that the procfs is deprecated, is that so? I get it that it may not be the best idea to use procfs for production modules, however there are still some kernel samples using that API. Also for debugging purposes it seems still usable. Are there alternatives I can use instead of procsfs? sysfs is not implemented either right?
For simple usages of sysfs you can mirror this: r.android.com/3342376
Though it'd be nice to abstract it properly.
Thank you! I will look into that. I get it that the sysfs isn't abstracted in the kernel either (yet).
The old RFL patch registry lists this for sysfs. It's from Jan 2023.
Thank you for the links. I will use the sysfs for my project, and will try to upstream it if I am satisfied with my abstractions.
